cluster_plan.label: Security / Compliance Response
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring vendor reviews, questionnaires, control mapping, and compliance-response loops.

cluster_plan.label: Healthcare Revenue Cycle
cluster_plan.focus: Appeals, denial management, reimbursement ops, and recurring healthcare admin loops.

cluster_plan.label: HVAC / Field Maintenance
cluster_plan.focus: Maintenance scheduling, dispatch, inspection, and recurring field-service optimization loops.

cluster_plan.label: Insurance / Claims / Appeals
cluster_plan.focus: Claim follow-up, dispute resolution, evidence packaging, and payout recovery loops.

cluster_plan.label: Vendor / Procurement Ops
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring vendor onboarding, procurement evidence, and buyer-side workflow loops.

cluster_plan.label: Legal / Audit / Evidence
cluster_plan.focus: Document-heavy compliance, evidence compilation, audit readiness, and legal-ops loops.

cluster_plan.label: Workplace Training / Compliance
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring workplace AI onboarding, reskilling, training compliance, and policy rollout loops.

cluster_plan.label: Industrial Quality / Inspection
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring inspections, defect logging, quality evidence, and plant-floor operations loops.

cluster_plan.label: Finance / Reconciliation / Backoffice
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring reconciliation, exception review, renewal risk, and backoffice finance loops.

cluster_plan.label: Logistics / Last Mile Ops
cluster_plan.focus: Recurring route planning, delivery exception handling, and logistics coordination loops.

agent_submissions.notes: Pilot agent 1 for Security / Compliance Response. Focus on repeated domain loops, not generic tooling.

agent_brief.focus: Recurring vendor reviews, questionnaires, control mapping, and compliance-response loops.
candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Security Control Crosswalk Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Builds and maintains a reusable control-and-evidence crosswalk across SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and similar frameworks.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: mapping one internal control baseline to multiple external framework requirements
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: reducing repeated evidence collection by learning reusable control crosswalks and evidence mappings
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: ingest a new framework request, map it to the control baseline, attach reusable evidence, and refine the crosswalk after auditor or customer review
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Hyperproof says teams repeat administrative work when they map frameworks one at a time and re-collect evidence, while ControlMap and practitioner discussion describe one control mapped to many frameworks with evidence reuse as the operational win.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Security and GRC teams keep rebuilding the same control-to-framework mapping and evidence matrix instead of operating a maintained crosswalk.

agent_submissions.notes: Pilot agent 2 for Security / Compliance Response. Focus on repeated domain loops, not generic tooling.

agent_brief.focus: Recurring vendor reviews, questionnaires, control mapping, and compliance-response loops.
candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Vendor Assertion Evidence Verifier
raw_candidates.description: Requests, validates, and tracks supporting artifacts behind vendor questionnaire claims for high-risk reviews.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: turning self-reported questionnaire answers into verified evidence packets for vendor risk teams
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which artifact types, freshness windows, and follow-ups actually resolve high-risk vendor assertions
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: parse vendor answers, request supporting evidence, verify artifact freshness and scope, and update the vendor evidence profile for future reviews
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: UpGuard and Safe Security both describe repetitive questionnaires, inconsistent responses, and verification difficulty as core TPRM bottlenecks, and Safe Security explicitly recommends requesting recent supporting artifacts for critical control assertions instead of trusting questionnaire answers alone.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Buyer-side security teams still spend manual cycles turning vendor attestations into evidence they can trust, especially for critical vendors.

agent_submissions.notes: Pilot agent 3 for Security / Compliance Response. Focus on repeated domain loops, not generic tooling.

agent_brief.focus: Recurring vendor reviews, questionnaires, control mapping, and compliance-response loops.
candidate_requirements: Return 1-3 candidate domain chips only
Every candidate must describe a repeated specialization loop
Every candidate must describe a repeated mastery loop
Reject personas, generic features, and vague workflow ideas

raw_candidates.label: Trust Center Gap Copilot
raw_candidates.description: Prepares the exact missing documents, report access workflows, and follow-up answers needed when a trust center does not fully satisfy a customer security review.
raw_candidates.specialization_surface: closing the gap between reusable trust-center content and bespoke customer security review asks
raw_candidates.mastery_surface: learning which trust-center gaps recur and how to answer them with minimal manual pre-sales and security effort
raw_candidates.user_value_loop: detect repeated customer asks, point to existing trust-center assets, package missing evidence, and update reusable trust-center content after each review
raw_candidates.evidence_summary: Cyberday positions trust centers as a way to cut repetitive security reviews, and practitioners still report that customers ask for the same evidence in different formats, leaving a recurring gap-handling layer after the trust center is in place.
raw_candidates.raw_observation: Even after SOC 2 and a trust center are in place, teams still need a repeatable way to handle the leftover spreadsheet, portal, and evidence-gap work.
